task_completion.description: Agent opens the maintenance group specifications document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window displaying the contents of 'fm_groups_spec.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Agent views the pre-loaded FZK-Haus model in the BIM software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender/Bonsai interface is visible, showing the 3D FZK-Haus architectural model.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the required IfcGroup entities based on the specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender/Bonsai interface shows the creation of new IFC Groups (e.g., Envelope, Access, Floor) in the relevant panel (such as Scene Properties under IFC Groups).

task_completion.description: Agent assigns the specified building elements to their respective maintenance groups.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows building elements (walls, windows, doors, slabs) selected in the 3D viewport or Outliner, and being assigned to the newly created IFC Groups.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the organized model as a new IFC project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project' or 'Export IFC' dialog box showing the file name 'fzk_fm_groups.ifc' being saved to the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the Blender/Bonsai GUI to perform grouping and assignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the graphical interface (mouse clicks, UI panels) to create groups and assign elements, rather than executing terminal commands or Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent actively selects elements and assigns them using the BIM software's grouping interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent selects actual 3D elements or Outliner items and uses the group assignment buttons/menus, rather than just renaming objects or typing text.

integrity.description: Agent uses the software's export/save feature to create the new IFC file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the 'Save' or 'Export' functionality within Blender/Bonsai, rather than copying the original file via the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification document is located at /home/ga/Desktop/fm_groups_spec.txt. The expected groups to be created are Envelope (external/facade), Access (doors/egress), and Floor (slabs/structural). The FZK-Haus model contains exactly 13 walls, 5 doors, 11 windows, and 4 slabs. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fm_groups.ifc.
